issues.description: Judge exit_clean for harden_only=true skips spec PR creation, .agent/ cleanup, and merge logic. The normal clean audit path (lines 641-705) creates a spec PR, cleans up .agent/ artifacts, and optionally auto-merges. The judge's exit_clean override just sets hardened_spec_path and transitions to Hardened, producing a terminal state with no PR deliverable. This means the hardened spec is never actually published as a PR.
issues.suggestion: Reuse the normal clean harden logic (or extract it into a helper like converge_review_clean) instead of inlining a simplified version. The exit_clean override should follow the same PR-creation and optional-merge flow as the normal v.clean==true path.

issues.description: FR-2 requires the judge context to include spec_content (full spec text), but invoke_judge_for_phase always sets spec_content: None. The spec explicitly defines the field as 'spec_content: "..." // full spec text'. The judge cannot properly evaluate whether functional requirements are met without the spec text — this degrades all three decision criteria (severity distribution, churn detection, reviewer drift).
issues.suggestion: Load spec content via self.git.read_file(&record.spec_path, &record.branch) and pass it as Some(content) in the JudgeContext. Handle the error gracefully (log warning and proceed with None if it fails).

issues.description: When the judge returns 'continue' with a hint in the harden phase, the hint is discarded. dispatch_revise hardcodes orchestrator_hint: None. In contrast, the review phase correctly extracts and passes the hint (line 1137). FR-3 says 'hint (when present) is written into the next round's feedback file' — this applies to both review and harden phases.
issues.suggestion: Thread the judge output's hint through to dispatch_revise. Either change dispatch_revise to accept an optional hint parameter, or extract the hint in evaluate_harden_stage and pass it along (similar to how evaluate_review_stage handles it at lines 1134-1143).

issues.description: The prompt is hardcoded in build_prompt() instead of loading the template from .nautiloop/prompts/judge.md. The spec lists this file as a deliverable and the file was created, but the code never reads it. This means editing the prompt template has no effect — the template is dead code. While the hardcoded prompt and the template are currently similar, they will inevitably drift.
issues.suggestion: Load the prompt template from .nautiloop/prompts/judge.md at startup or on first use, substituting {{CONTEXT}} with the serialized context JSON. Fall back to the hardcoded prompt if the file is missing.

issues.description: Misleading comment 'verdict_clean && round > 1: still invoke to log the decision' followed by returning None (which does NOT invoke the judge). The comment describes intent that was never implemented, creating confusion for future maintainers.
issues.suggestion: Either remove the comment and add a clear one like '// verdict_clean && round > 1: no trigger applies, skip judge', or implement the described behavior if it was intended.

issues.description: confidence is stored as f64 in JudgeOutput but cast to f32 in JudgeDecisionRecord via `output.confidence.map(|c| c as f32)`. This silent precision loss could produce different confidence values in logs (f64) vs database (f32).
issues.suggestion: Use f64 consistently, or use f32 consistently. Since the database column is REAL (f32), using f32 in JudgeOutput would be simplest.

issues.description: FR-6c says 'nemo inspect output gains a judge_decisions array... so engineers can see why the loop made non-obvious transitions.' The CLI just dumps raw JSON. While the data is present in the output, there's no formatted rendering of judge decisions inline with rounds as the spec envisions. This is acceptable for Stage 1 but worth noting.
issues.suggestion: Consider adding formatted output for judge_decisions alongside round summaries in a future pass.
